name: Pico Gilbo Loop Trail
name_original: Pico Gilbo (1.679 m) – Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre
description: Hike the challenging Pico Gilbo, often called the 'Leonese Matterhorn,' for panoramic views of the Riaño reservoir and surrounding mountains
region_intro: Hiking around Embalse de Riaño offers trails through dramatic landscapes within the Cantabrian Mountains, often referred to as the "Leonese fjords" due to the reservoir's winding waters through steep terrain. The region features a diverse topography, including high peaks like Pico Gilbo, deep river valleys, and extensive Atlantic forests with oak and beech trees. Hikers can explore varied environments, from reservoir-side paths to mountain ascents, all set against a backdrop of rugged peaks. Best hiking trails around Embalse de Riaño The most popular hiking route is Pico Gilbo Loop Trail, a 5.0 miles (8.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 35 minutes to complete. This difficult route ascends the iconic Pico Gilbo, offering panoramic views of the reservoir and surrounding mountains. Another top favourite among local hikers is Views of the Riaño Reservoir – Cave of the Old Woman of the Mountain loop from Riaño, a moderate 4.1 miles (6.7 km) path. This trail provides scenic views of the reservoir and passes by a notable local landmark. Local hikers also love the Riaño Swing – Valcayo Viewpoint loop from Riaño, a 4.2 miles (6.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain with viewpoints, often completed in about 1 hour 53 minutes. Hiking around Embalse de Riaño is defined by its fjord-like reservoir, the majestic Cantabrian Mountains, and deep river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Embalse de Riaño are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 350 reviews. More than 2,300 hikers have used komoot to explore Embalse de Riaño's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Embalse de Riaño?
A: There are 11 hiking trails around Embalse de Riaño, offering a range of experiences from easy strolls to challenging mountain ascents. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars.

Q: Are there any easy hiking routes suitable for beginners in Embalse de Riaño?
A: Yes, Embalse de Riaño offers 2 easy hiking routes perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed walk. One popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14990153" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valcayo Viewpoint – Riaño Swing loop from Riaño&lt;/a&gt;, which provides scenic views with a manageable elevation gain.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Embalse de Riaño?
A: Hiking in Embalse de Riaño reveals dramatic and diverse landscapes. You'll encounter the stunning 'Leonese fjords' where the reservoir's waters wind through steep, mountainous terrain. The region is surrounded by the majestic Cantabrian Mountains, with peaks like Pico Gilbo, deep river valleys, and extensive Atlantic forests featuring oak and beech trees. You might also discover interesting glacial lakes.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Embalse de Riaño?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Embalse de Riaño are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050074263/pico-gilbo-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Gilbo Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; offers a challenging circular ascent with panoramic views, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38170916" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Riaño Reservoir – Cave of the Old Woman of the Mountain loop from Riaño&lt;/a&gt; provides a moderate circular option with scenic reservoir vistas.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints or attractions along the hiking trails?
A: The region is rich with stunning viewpoints and unique attractions. You can enjoy panoramic vistas from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6308896" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Riaño Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or the Valcayo Viewpoint. Don't miss the Riaño Swing, reportedly Spain's largest, offering exhilarating views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40306192" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Riaño Reservoir – Las Viescas Recreation Area loop from Riaño&lt;/a&gt; also offers great sights.

Q: Can I find family-friendly hiking options in Embalse de Riaño?
A: While some routes are challenging, there are options suitable for families. The easier trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14990155" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riaño Swing – Valcayo Viewpoint loop from Riaño&lt;/a&gt;, are generally well-suited for families looking for a pleasant walk with rewarding views without excessive difficulty.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Embalse de Riaño?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 350 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the dramatic 'Leonese fjords' scenery, the majestic mountain backdrops, and the diverse trails that cater to various fitness levels. The unique viewpoints and the iconic Pico Gilbo are also often highlighted as memorable experiences.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or cultural sites to explore near the trails?
A: Yes, beyond the natural beauty, you can explore cultural landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5443521" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hórreo of Salio - Riaño&lt;/a&gt; offers a glimpse into traditional architecture. The Paseo del Recuerdo (Memory Trail) is a poignant walk with information panels about the villages submerged by the reservoir, offering both history and excellent views. The Ethnographic Museum in Riaño town also provides insights into local life.

Q: Is it possible to hike with dogs in Embalse de Riaño?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in Embalse de Riaño, especially on public paths. However, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, particularly in protected areas like the Riaño and Mampodre Mountain Regional Park, to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Always check local regulations for specific routes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Embalse de Riaño?
A: Embalse de Riaño offers beautiful hiking experiences throughout the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant colors, with lush greenery in spring and stunning foliage in autumn. Summer is ideal for higher altitude trails, while winter can offer unique snow-covered landscapes, though some routes may require specific gear or be inaccessible.

Q: Are there opportunities for long-distance hiking or multi-day treks?
A: While the provided routes are generally day hikes, the surrounding Cantabrian Mountains and the Riaño and Mampodre Mountain Regional Park offer extensive terrain for longer adventures. The dramatic peaks and diverse ecosystems suggest that experienced hikers could link several trails or explore more remote areas for multi-day treks, though specific long-distance routes are not detailed here.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in Embalse de Riaño?
A: Yes, for many popular hiking routes around Embalse de Riaño, parking is generally available near the trailheads, especially in and around the town of Riaño. It's common to find designated parking areas, particularly for routes that start from accessible points like the town center or well-known viewpoints.
tips: The Mirador de Las Biescas, located near Riaño (León), is one of the most spectacular spots in the Riaño and Mampodre Mountain Regional Park. It's ideal for a simple hike, with stunning views and a touch of legend.

Pico Gilbo is an iconic mountain located in the Cantabrian Mountains, near Riaño, in the province of León. With an altitude of 1,679 metres, it is known for its stunning panoramic views and challenging climb.

faqs.question: How difficult is the hike to Pico Gilbo?
faqs.answer: The Pico Gilbo trail is generally rated as difficult. While some sections are moderate, the final push to the summit is particularly demanding. It involves steep ascents, vertical and exposed areas, and minor scrambles. The descent can also be tricky due to steepness and loose ground. It is not recommended for individuals prone to vertigo.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Pico Gilbo trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. You'll start on tracks near the Riaño reservoir, transition into mountain paths, and pass through beautiful beech forests. The upper reaches of Pico Gilbo become considerably rockier, with sections of loose stones, scree, and exposed areas. The final ascent to the summit involves steep climbs and minor scrambles.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers spectacular panoramic views throughout. Key highlights include the stunning 'Leonese Fjords', the turquoise waters of the Riaño reservoir, and a magnificent backdrop of surrounding mountains like the Rionda Rock and the Picos de Europa in the distance. Don't miss the View of Pico Gilbo, Pico Yordas, and Riaño Reservoir from Mirador Las Breñas Dondes Macho and the rewarding 360-degree panorama from Gilbo Peak itself.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike Pico Gilbo?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike Pico Gilbo is typically during the warmer, drier months, from late spring to early autumn. This avoids snow and ice, which can make the already challenging terrain much more hazardous, especially on the exposed summit sections. Always check local weather conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Pico Gilbo trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details aren't provided, access points for the Pico Gilbo trail are generally found near the Riaño reservoir within the Regional Park Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre. It's advisable to research specific trailhead parking areas in Riaño or nearby villages before your visit.

faqs.question: Is the Pico Gilbo trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information regarding dogs on this specific trail is not explicitly stated. However, as it's within a Regional Park, dogs are generally permitted but must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers, especially given the challenging and exposed sections of the trail.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for hiking in Regional Park Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for day hiking on trails within the Regional Park Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre. However, always respect park regulations, which may include rules about camping, fires, and waste disposal.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with other paths. You may find yourself on parts of the Camino Vadiniense and the Camino de las Viescas, as well as the Canal Moro.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike up Pico Gilbo?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and varied terrain, you should bring sturdy hiking boots with good ankle support, plenty of water, snacks, appropriate layers of clothing for changing mountain weather, sun protection, and a map or GPS device. Trekking poles can be very helpful for the steep ascents and descents, especially on loose ground.

faqs.question: Is the Pico Gilbo trail a loop or an out-and-back?
faqs.answer: The Komoot tour for Pico Gilbo is typically an out-and-back route, meaning you return along the same path after reaching the summit. This allows you to experience the challenging ascent and descent on the same demanding sections.

faqs.question: What makes Pico Gilbo unique compared to other peaks in the region?
faqs.answer: Pico Gilbo is often dubbed the 'Leonese Matterhorn' due to its striking pyramidal silhouette, making it an iconic and easily recognizable peak. Its position also offers unique perspectives of the Riaño reservoir and its viaduct, set against the backdrop of the Regional Park Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre, which serves as an antechamber to the Picos de Europa.

waypoints.name: View of Pico Gilbo, Pico Yordas, and Riaño Reservoir from Mirador Las Breñas Dondes Macho
